goal: Given an address and project type, predict how long the permit approval process is likely to take and surface key factors driving that timeline so applicants can compare options and reduce delay risk before submitting.

root: permit_lookup.json — spatial grid index of historical permits for history card
api/: main.py — FastAPI backend serving quantile model predictions
data/: building_permits.csv — full raw Seattle permits (post-2000 used for permit_lookup)
plan_comments.csv — 26,299 rows, 707 unique permits (partial public export)
comment_features.csv — per-permit comment features extracted by CommentFeatures.ipynb
output/: feature_selection_results.csv — ranked feature recommendations from SHAP analysis
notebooks/: CommentFeatures.ipynb — comment feature extraction from plan_comments.csv
FeatureSelection.ipynb — two-stage MI + SHAP feature selection
PermitPredictor_RF.ipynb — broad RF model (focused SF/ADU/DADU version also exists)
Corrections-Letters-v5.ipynb — PDF correction letter extraction pipeline
convert_to_onnx.ipynb — converts joblib to ONNX (used for older RF models, not quantile)
scripts/: generate_permit_lookup.py — rebuilds permit_lookup.json from full building_permits.csv

plan_comments.source: Seattle Open Data Portal — PARTIAL EXPORT

modeling_population.notes: 9060 completed permits missing review time excluded — field inspection pathway (no recorded review time)

liaison_guidance.estprojectcost: EXCLUDED — not known by applicant at submission time
liaison_guidance.dwellingunittype: EXCLUDED from modeling — inaccurate data. OK to use as population filter only
liaison_guidance.housingcategory: EXCLUDED — assigned post-build, not available at submission
liaison_guidance.reviewpriority: Non-default entries excluded from modeling population
liaison_guidance.housingunitsadded/removed/net: Use these instead of housingcategory for unit counts
liaison_guidance.plan_comments_coverage: Low coverage due to partial export. Full dataset available via Corrections-Letters-v5.ipynb PDF extraction pipeline
liaison_guidance.field_inspection_permits: Completed permits with null review time are small projects subject to field inspection — complete in less than a day

production.name: Quantile LightGBM
production.architecture: Three LightGBM models (alpha=0.10, 0.50, 0.90) with quantile loss. Preprocessor + models stored as bundle dict.
production.preprocessing: OrdinalEncoder for categoricals, passthrough for numerics (LightGBM handles nulls natively)

metrics.note: Coverage = fraction of actual values within optimistic-pessimistic interval

production.review_complexity_max_note: VERIFY WITH LIAISON — confirm this is assigned at submission time not during review

feature_selection.method: Two-stage: Mutual Information filter (5% of max threshold) then SHAP analysis (1% norm threshold) using LightGBM

website.stack: GitHub Pages (static frontend) + Render free tier (FastAPI backend)
website.geocoder: Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) — Census geocoder blocked by CORS
website.zone_inference: Geographic heuristics from lat/lon — not a true spatial join

history_card.source: permit_lookup.json — spatial grid index built from full building_permits.csv post-2000

render.plan: free (512MB RAM, spins down after inactivity)
render.coldstart: ~30 seconds, handled by warmup ping on page load with retry logic

files_not_needed_on_render: model_meta.json — ONNX approach abandoned in favor of FastAPI
inputs_removed: estprojectcost — not known at submission per liaison

comment_extraction.status: Pipeline built, do_lookup/do_download/do_parsing flags set to False by default
stages: do_lookup: hits city ECM API for correction letter URLs
comment_extraction.filter: Filtered to 14k modeling population permits only
comment_extraction.known_issue: Public plan_comments.csv is full export (26,299 rows) — no additional data available via API. Full coverage requires liaison-provided internal export.
comment_extraction.building_permits_column: PermitNum renamed to RecordNumber in notebook

open_questions: review_complexity_max: confirm with liaison whether assigned at submission or during review
Full plan_comments dataset: request complete internal export from liaison to improve comment feature coverage from 5% to potentially 30-40%
Temporal leakage: KFold shuffle means future permits can train on past — time-based split would be more rigorous for production
ONNX conversion: skl2onnx does not support LightGBM quantile objective — FastAPI is the correct production path
next_steps: Run Corrections-Letters-v5.ipynb once liaison provides fuller comment data
Retrain PermitPredictor_Quantile.ipynb with enriched comment features
Verify review_complexity_max leakage status with liaison
Consider time-based train/val split for more honest evaluation
Update permit_lookup.json after any master_dataset changes by running scripts/generate_permit_lookup.py

paths.desktop_data: C:/Users/flori/Desktop/data — PDF downloads from correction letter extraction

preferences.formatting: prose paragraphs preferred over bullet points
preferences.acronyms: always spell out acronyms (e.g. RF = Random Forest, MAE = Mean Absolute Error, OOF = Out-of-Fold, SHAP = SHapley Additive exPlanations, ADU = Accessory Dwelling Unit, DADU = Detached Accessory Dwelling Unit, MI = Mutual Information, CV = Cross Validation)
preferences.code_style: Times New Roman not applicable to code; filenames include descriptive titles
preferences.report_style: diagnostic reports at end of each notebook, plain text format
